questions.question_text: A child's scores for her spelling tests were 7, 1, 2, 5, 2, 3 and 9. What is the median of these numbers?
questions.explanation: Ordered: 1, 2, 2, 3, 5, 7, 9. The middle (4th) value is 3. (4.14 is the mean, not the median.)

questions.question_text: A study recorded the performance of premiers in the last four matches of each regular season: 56 wins, 15 losses and 1 draw in total. How many past seasons of competition are included?
questions.explanation: Total matches = 56 + 15 + 1 = 72. Each season contributes 4 matches: 72 ÷ 4 = 18 seasons.

questions.question_text: In a golf competition, five golfers scored 76, 74, 85, 72 and 73 at Course X and 69, 72, 76, 74 and 74 at Course Y. The organisers want to adjust each golfer's Course Y score so the five adjusted scores have the same mean as the Course X scores. Which change is needed?
questions.explanation: Course X mean = 380/5 = 76. Course Y mean = 365/5 = 73. Each Y score must increase by 3.

answers.text: Increase each golfer's result for Course Y by 3
Decrease each golfer's result for Course Y by 2
Increase each golfer's result for Course Y by 2
Decrease each golfer's result for Course Y by 3

questions.question_text: At a school, Year 11 students had these Unit 3 and 4 enrolments: 77 in Biology, 61 in General Mathematics and 29 in other subjects (167 enrolments in total). Students who chose two subjects studied both Biology and General Mathematics, no one studied more than two, and 16 students studied both. How many Year 11 students studied at least one Unit 3 and 4 subject?
questions.explanation: 167 enrolments count the 16 double-subject students twice: 167 − 16 = 151 students.

questions.question_text: The bar chart shows the favourite subjects of the Year 8 students interviewed. What percentage of students prefers Maths to all other subjects in the survey?
questions.explanation: Reading the bars: English 7, Maths 9, Science 7, Language 5, History 4, Geography 4, Music 2, Art 2 — total 40. Maths = 9 ÷ 40 = 22.5%.

questions.question_text: The pie chart shows the votes for four candidates for school captain. Which statement is true?
questions.explanation: Candidate A's sector is about half the circle, so A won with roughly 50% of the votes. A pie chart shows only proportions, not turnout, and D is the smallest sector so it cannot be tied for the win.

answers.text: Candidate A won with approximately 50% of the votes.
Only 90% of the students in the school voted.
Candidates C and D were tied for the winning number of votes.
Candidate B received twice as many votes as Candidates C and D combined.

questions.question_text: The pie chart shows how a student spends their time after school. Which two activities in total take up half the student's time?
questions.explanation: Reading the 30°-tick sectors: studying = 150° and playing video games = 30°, together 180° — exactly half of the 360° circle.

answers.text: playing video games and watching favourite shows

questions.question_text: On the after-school pie chart the student exercises for 55 minutes. What is the total time the student spends on activities after school?
questions.explanation: The exercising sector spans 60° (two 30° ticks) = 60/360 = 1/6 of the time. So the total is 55 × 6 = 330 min = 5 h 30 min.

questions.question_text: The mosaic plot shows favourite subjects of 160 students and the teachers surveyed. What percentage of the students said PE?
questions.explanation: In the students column PE runs from 45% to 100% on the scale, so PE = 55% of the students.

questions.question_text: On the mosaic plot the column widths are proportional to the number of people, with 160 students and (from the 4 : 1 width ratio) 40 teachers. What percentage of all the people surveyed said English?
questions.explanation: Students who said English: 10% × 160 = 16. Teachers who said English: 45% × 40 = 18. Total English = 34 of 200 people = 17%.

questions.question_text: On the mosaic plot 20 more students are surveyed and added to the 160. What is the greatest possible percentage of the students whose favourite subject is Science?
questions.explanation: Science was 25% × 160 = 40 students. At most all 20 new students pick Science: 60 of 180 = 33%.
